resources.string: 使用している物理キーボードの配列を選択します。
物理キーボードショートカット
Ctrl+C、変換キー、無変換キー、Fキーなどにアクションを割り当てます。
物理キーボードショートカット
ショートカットがありません。
同じキー割り当てが既に登録されています。

On this screen, you can freely register words that you want to appear in the keyboard predictive conversion.\nRegistered words will appear as conversion candidates on the keyboard once you type at least the number of characters specified in their "reading".

You can register email addresses, code snippets, Markdown tables, and more with short "readings". When you type the registered "reading" exactly on the keyboard, the full registered text will appear as a conversion candidate.

Examples:
- Reading: myemail
 Template: my.long.email.address@example.com
- Reading: html5
 Template: <!DOCTYPE html><html>…</html>
You can add or remove keyboards
Adjust the corner radius of the keyboard background.
When the device is in landscape, temporarily switch to a QWERTY keyboard.
Use Japanese mode with Romaji conversion when switching to QWERTY in landscape.
Use English mode when switching to QWERTY in landscape.
Height adjustment for certain devices is enabled (e.g., Galaxy).
Height adjustment for certain devices is disabled.
Set the maximum number of conversion candidates to display.
Number of columns for conversion candidates
Number of columns for conversion candidates(Landscape)
Set the number of columns when displaying conversion candidates.
Please select the number of candidate columns.
Setting for the height of the View that displays conversion candidates.
Please select the height of the View that displays conversion candidates.
Don\'t show conversion candidates while typing a password
Show conversion candidates while typing a password
Treat as pre-edit text during password input.
Treat as committed text during password input.
Delete key action on highlighted candidate
Long press Delete: Unhighlight\nTap Delete: Move selection back one place
You can register words by long-pressing a conversion candidate.
Use only flick input and disable toggle input
Adds a \"Restore Reading\" button that restores the last committed reading to pre-edit.
Always use half-width space regardless of the input mode
Start live conversion after this many characters are entered.
Omit modifier key input is enabled\ne.g., "かつこう" → "がっこう"
Omit modifier key input is disabled
Adjust how long a key must be held before long-press is triggered.
Create and select a Romaji conversion table
Show a window when a key is detected.
Do not show a window when a key is detected.
Show cursor buttons on the QWERTY keyboard.
Do not show cursor buttons on the QWERTY keyboard.
Show punctuation (、。) buttons on the QWERTY keyboard.
Do not show punctuation (、。) buttons on the QWERTY keyboard.
Show the keyboard switch (globe icon) button.
Do not show the keyboard switch (globe icon) button.
Show the emoji button on the QWERTY keyboard.
Do not show the emoji button on the QWERTY keyboard.
Select the initial screen for the symbol keyboard
Choose the skin tone used for skin-tone-compatible emoji.
Automatically commits when a custom key outputs 2 or more characters.
Does not auto-commit even when a custom key outputs 2 or more characters.
Display when no text is entered
Show a list from the custom tab when no text is entered.
Keep the standard display (clipboard, etc.) when no text is entered.
Shows the custom keyboard you used last when opening custom keyboards
Shows the first custom keyboard when opening custom keyboards
Leave blank to use the default margin.
Enter a number greater than or equal to 0.
Time to wait for the next input in toggle mode (milliseconds)
Vibrates when a key is tapped
Does not vibrate when a key is tapped
Plays a sound when a key is pressed
Does not play a sound when a key is pressed
Display the list of words to hide
Enable learning dictionary\nShows learned conversion candidates
Disable learning dictionary\nDoes not show learned conversion candidates
Enable predictive conversion for user dictionary\nShows registered words as predictive candidates
Disable predictive conversion for user dictionary
Enable templates\nShows registered words in conversion candidates
Enable long-press translation for conversion candidates when a Gemma 4 model is loaded
Create reusable prompts and show them in the candidate long-press menu
No Gemma prompt templates yet. Add one to reuse it from the candidate long-press menu.
Title shown in the long-press menu
Prompt to apply to the selected candidate
This prompt template will be removed from the long-press menu.
No Gemma 4 model has been imported yet.
Gemma translation requires a 64-bit device (arm64-v8a or x86_64).
Please select the Gemma 4 E2B LiteRT-LM model file.
The selected file is too small to be a valid Gemma model.
The selected file does not look like a valid LiteRT-LM model.
Translation was cancelled because the input text changed.
Adds a dictionary created by extracting words common to both the latest Wikipedia and Neologd.
Adds a dictionary created by extracting words found only in the latest Wikipedia.
Adds a dictionary created by extracting words found only in Neologd.
Shows words registered in the learn dictionary as conversion candidates with a prefix match of %1$d characters
Shows words registered in the user dictionary as conversion candidates with a prefix match of %1$d characters
Enables the ability to undo when you delete committed text
Disables the ability to undo when you delete committed text
Currently in half-width input mode.\nFlick left for a full-width space
Currently in full-width input mode.\nFlick left for a half-width space
Show the symbol keymap on long press in the QWERTY keyboard.
Do not show the symbol keymap on long press in the QWERTY keyboard.
Switch to QWERTY keyboard when entering a password.
Keep using the ten key when entering a password.
Use QWERTY layout for alphabet input mode
Show the keyboard as a movable floating keyboard
Delete all learned dictionaries.\nThis action cannot be undone. Are you sure?
\nAre you sure to delete the word?
Import failed. The file format is incorrect.
The default map cannot be edited.
There is no keymap to export.
will be deleted.This action cannot be undone.
This will delete all registered templates. This action cannot be undone. Are you sure?
Could not find templates to export.
That word and reading are already registered.
Please enter the word and its reading.
There is no word to export.
This will delete all registered words. This action cannot be undone. Are you sure?
Choose initial screen in symbol keyboard.
Are you sure to delete all clipboard history?
Display tabs at the top of the candidate view to switch between "Prediction", "Conversion", and "Alphanumeric/Kana".
Show a toolbar with useful shortcuts like switching Emoji keyboard
Show a key (あa) to switch between Romaji input and English input
Choose the keyboard theme color.\nTo apply changes, please switch the keyboard once.
When you flick the delete key to the left, it deletes the text or symbols before the cursor.
When you flick the delete key upward, it deletes the text or symbols after the cursor.
When you flick the delete key downward, it restores the last deletion (Undo).
The delete key left flick stops at these symbols. Spaces and line breaks are always treated as word boundaries.
At least one symbol must remain.
Configure the 2-character pairs that move the cursor between them after commit.
If committed pre-edit text exactly matches one of these 2-character pairs, the cursor moves left once so it lands between them.
No pairs registered. When empty, the cursor will not auto-move.
Adjust the size of the characters shown on the conversion candidates.
Adjust the height of the candidate bar
Show clipboard contents in the conversion bar
When text or images are copied to the clipboard, show them in the conversion bar.
When on, all four corners of the keyboard are rounded.
Split the composing text into segments and select the first one.
While segment conversion is active, move the focused segment with the left and right cursor keys.
When composing text, hold down the space key to move the cursor.
Change the display order of candidate tabs
Makes only the first letter after Shift uppercase for consistency in all cases
Tap to remove from candidate bar
When you tap a clipboard item in the candidate bar, it will be removed from the bar.
When enabled, clipboard items that are not pinned are deleted after the selected number of hours.
Choose how many hours to keep unpinned clipboard items.
Capitalize only the first character after Shift
In romaji input, only the first character after pressing the Shift key becomes uppercase.
Use the kana (Gojūon) layout on tablets.
Thank you for downloading Sumire. To use this app, you must first enable it in your device\'s keyboard settings.
Allows to use upward flick input on QWERTY keys.
Input uppercase letters with downward flick.
Enable predictive text conversion using the neural kana-kanji model “zenz-v3.1-xsmall.”
Set a profile that zenz will use for predictive conversion.\nExample: If you enter “Occupation: Doctor,” typing “chūsha” will make the candidate “injection” appear more frequently.
Uses zenz to lightly reorder the top conversion candidates. The existing dictionary score is still used together with the zenz score.
Add AI suggestions on Convert-key long press
When detecting long-press the Convert key, add conversion suggestions from the zenz neural kana-kanji model.
Switch keyboards for this setting to take effect.
Sets the maximum number of contexts zenz processes at once.\nIn practice, it converts up to roughly “contexts × 1.4” characters, and any characters beyond this limit are discarded.

Sets the maximum number of tokens zenz can use in a single conversion, including left context and input. Increasing this value allows Zenz to handle longer context, but may increase memory usage and slow down inference.
Number of threads used by zenz
Sets the number of CPU threads used for zenz inference. Higher values can make conversions faster, but may increase CPU usage and battery drain. Adjust this according to your device performance.
Open the screen to edit the icons shown on the toolbar.
When enabled, the space key on the romaji (QWERTY) keyboard inserts a full-width space.
When enabled, numbers typed on the Romaji QWERTY keyboard are inserted as half-width characters.
When enabled, symbols typed on the Romaji QWERTY keyboard are inserted as half-width characters.
Displays guide characters on the keys to indicate how to input each character.
Displays the tap character in the center and flick guide characters around flick keys.
Adjusts the size of the characters displayed on the keys.
Add Romaji conversion suggestions for Hiragana input
Learn when the top conversion suggestion is selected
Enable predictive suggestions using the learned dictionary
Input mode when switching from the number keyboard
When switching to the Kana keyboard or Sumire keyboard, switch to Japanese input mode instead of the number keyboard.。
Automatically shows the keyboard you used last time when the app restarts.
If there is no text to the left of the cursor, conversion candidates are generated using the context on the right.
If a typo is detected while using flick input, corrected conversion candidates are shown.
If a typo is detected while using a QWERTY keyboard, corrected candidates are shown.
When typing correction is enabled, use the corrected input string as the input to zenz.
Save your current settings as a backup file.
Restore settings from a backup file.
Behavior of Full-Width n in Custom Romaji
When using custom romaji, enabling this option will convert a full-width n to \"ん\" when another character is entered after it
Sets the correction strength for typing mistakes in Japanese flick input. Higher values make the correction less aggressive.(Default: 3000)
Sets the correction strength for input entered without modifier keys. Higher values make the correction less aggressive. (Default: 1900)
Adjust the size of the text shown on keys and the size of the keys.\nThis setting also applies to the custom keyboard.
Adjust the size of the text shown on keys and the size of the keys.\nThis setting also applies to the Sumire keyboard.
Create a system user dictionary from user-defined words
No built dictionary file has been saved yet.
`score` must fit within the Short range.
Please select valid leftId/rightId values from id.def.
Delete all system user dictionary entries?
Failed to export system user dictionary.
System user dictionary was loaded (%1$d entries).
Failed to load system user dictionary.
Imported %1$d entries from external dictionary (%2$d lines skipped). Tap Build Dictionary to apply.
No importable entries were found (skipped %1$d lines).
Imported %1$d entries (%2$d skipped). Tap Build Dictionary to apply.
Failed to delete built dictionary files.
Add N-gram rules to adjust conversion candidate
Choose an image from your files to use as the keyboard background.
Choose how to display the background image.
Remove the selected image and restore the theme background.
Keyboard background image has been set.
Keyboard background image has been cleared.
Failed to set keyboard background image: %1$s
Choose a video from your files to use as the keyboard background.
Choose the playback quality for the background video.
Remove the selected video and restore image or theme background.
Keyboard background video has been set.
Keyboard background video has been cleared.
Failed to set keyboard background video: %1$s
